FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class CommandBarFlyout

Namespace
FluentGpu.Controls
Assembly
FluentGpu.Controls.dll

A WinUI CommandBarFlyout: a trigger button that opens a contextual command toolbar anchored below it. The popup has a horizontal PRIMARY row of icon AppBarButtons plus a trailing "…" More ellipsis toggle, and a vertical SECONDARY overflow menu of labeled rows that the … toggle expands. 1:1 with the V2 sources controls/dev/CommandBarFlyout/CommandBarFlyout_themeresources.xaml + CommandBarFlyoutCommandBar.cpp:

<ul><li>Primary buttons: MinWidth 40 ContentRoot (:285), InnerBorderMargin 2 (:107), 16px icon, subtle fill ramp
with the 83ms BrushTransition (:282) on every state swap. CHECKED primary toggles paint the accent pill
(Checked/CheckedPointerOver/CheckedPressed, :386-406; brushes :24-:30) with on-accent foreground; CheckedDisabled
drops the pill (:407-416). Primary clicks do NOT auto-close (closeFlyoutFunc is hooked only on SecondaryCommands,
CommandBarFlyout.cpp:67-74).</li><li>Overflow rows: plate inset 2 (InnerBorderMargin :107) inside the presenter's 3px ItemsPresenter margin
(:556); check glyph E73E @12 (:505); 16×16 menu icon; OverflowTextLabel Body 14 Padding 0,6,0,7 at the 12/39/67
lead ladder (:301/:152/:180); KeyboardAcceleratorTextLabel Caption 12 Margin 24,0,12,0 (:302); SubItemChevron
E76C @12 (:303). CHECKED overflow toggles show ONLY the check glyph — no accent pill (OverflowChecked sets just
OverflowCheckGlyph.Opacity=1, :434-438; hover/press stay the Subtle ramp, :439-458). Every secondary command —
toggles included — closes the flyout on invoke (Click/Checked/Unchecked → Hide(), CommandBarFlyout.cpp:97-105).</li><li>Expand/collapse: the V2 ExpansionStates transitions (themeresources:697-857) — the overflow reveals through
a HALF-HEIGHT CLIP slide with static content (ExpandDownAnimationStartPosition = −h/2,
CommandBarFlyoutCommandBar.cpp:891; ExpandedUp from +h/2, :888), synced with the WIDTH expansion (content clip
right edge from the midpoint, WidthExpansionAnimationStartPosition = −delta/2, :922; MoreButton glide delta/2→0,
:973) — 250ms open / 167ms close on ControlFastOutSlowInKeySpline 0,0,0,1. ExpandedUp places the overflow ABOVE
the primary row with flipped seam/corners (:966-973) when the popup is bottom-anchored (shouldExpandUp,
:609-657).</li><li>Flyout open/close: an 83ms opacity fade (Opening/ClosingOpacityStoryboard, themeresources:655-662) — the
menu clip-unfold is explicitly disabled (AreOpenCloseAnimationsEnabled(false), CommandBarFlyout.cpp:44).</li></ul>

The flyout body returns INNER content only — <xref href="FluentGpu.Controls.OverlayHost" data-throw-if-not-resolved="false"></xref>'s FlyoutSurface supplies the acrylic
backdrop, 1px stroke, shadow and rounded corners. The popup is WINDOWED (ShouldConstrainToRootBounds=False,
CommandBarFlyout.cpp:43).

public sealed class CommandBarFlyout : Component

Component

Fields

AlwaysExpanded

WinUI V2 AlwaysExpanded: keep the overflow menu shown and hide the … More button. Forces Standard (CommandBarFlyout.cpp:173-177).

public bool AlwaysExpanded

Field Value

bool

PartMoreButton

The trailing … ellipsis expand toggle (WinUI MoreButton). Owned: OnClick (expand/collapse), Role, OnKeyDown (the arrow-key roving), OnRealized (the width-expansion glide node capture, chained).

public const string PartMoreButton = "MoreButton"

Field Value

string

PartOverflow

The overflow region of labeled rows (WinUI OverflowContentRoot). Owned: OnRealized (the expand clip storyboard's node capture, chained), ClipToBounds, Children.

public const string PartOverflow = "Overflow"

Field Value

string

PartPrimaryRow

The horizontal primary icon row (WinUI PrimaryItemsRoot). Owned: Children.

public const string PartPrimaryRow = "PrimaryRow"

Field Value

string

PartRoot

The popup body root. Owned: ClipToBounds (the expand clips must never paint past the popup's rounded corners), Children, OnRealized (the width-expansion clip + open/close fade node capture, chained).

public const string PartRoot = "Root"

Field Value

string

PartTrigger

The trigger button that opens the flyout (engine-local — WinUI flyouts have no built-in trigger). Owned: OnClick (the open toggle), Role, OnRealized (the popup anchor capture, chained).

public const string PartTrigger = "Trigger"

Field Value

string

Parts

Lightweight per-part styling (CSS ::part): modifiers keyed by the PartXxx consts; see TemplateParts for the contract. Threaded into the popup body each open.

public TemplateParts? Parts

Field Value

TemplateParts

Placement 

public FlyoutPlacement Placement

Field Value

FlyoutPlacement

PrimaryCommands 

public IReadOnlyList<AppBarCommand> PrimaryCommands

Field Value

IReadOnlyList<AppBarCommand>

SecondaryCommands 

public IReadOnlyList<AppBarCommand> SecondaryCommands

Field Value

IReadOnlyList<AppBarCommand>

ShowMode

Standard = open expanded (no transition) + focus the first command; Transient = open collapsed, no focus steal (CommandBarFlyout.cpp:162-201; CommandBarFlyoutCommandBar.cpp:29-71).

public CommandBarFlyoutShowMode ShowMode

Field Value

CommandBarFlyoutShowMode

TriggerLabel 

public string TriggerLabel

Field Value

string

Methods

Create(string)

Back-compat shim: the zero/one-arg form still works, supplying a representative sample command set so existing call sites (and the gallery) keep compiling.

public static Element Create(string triggerLabel = "Commands")

Parameters

triggerLabel string

Returns

Element

Create(string, IReadOnlyList<AppBarCommand>, IReadOnlyList<AppBarCommand>, bool, FlyoutPlacement, CommandBarFlyoutShowMode) 

public static Element Create(string triggerLabel, IReadOnlyList<AppBarCommand> primaryCommands, IReadOnlyList<AppBarCommand> secondaryCommands, bool alwaysExpanded = false, FlyoutPlacement placement = FlyoutPlacement.BottomLeft, CommandBarFlyoutShowMode showMode = CommandBarFlyoutShowMode.Transient)

Parameters

triggerLabel string
primaryCommands IReadOnlyList<AppBarCommand>
secondaryCommands IReadOnlyList<AppBarCommand>
alwaysExpanded bool
placement FlyoutPlacement
showMode CommandBarFlyoutShowMode

Returns

Element

Render()

Returns

Element
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
